assertion_results.text: Version impact table shows mixed results: 2.1.x versions marked YES (affected) and 2.2.x versions marked NO (not affected) based on lock file evidence
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md table shows 2.1.0 and 2.1.1 as YES (h2 0.4.5 < 0.4.8) and all 2.2.x versions (2.2.0-2.2.4) as NO (h2 >= 0.4.8). Lock file evidence cited via Cargo.lock at pinned source commits.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction includes only the actually affected versions (2.1.x versions), not the unaffected 2.2.x versions (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md proposes Affects Versions as only 'RHTPA 2.1.0' and 'RHTPA 2.1.1'. RHTPA 2.2.0 is explicitly removed with reason 'Not affected: ships h2 0.4.8 (the fixed version)'. No 2.2.x versions remain.

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks are created only for the affected stream (2.1.x), not for 2.2.x which already ships the patched version (Step 8 Case A scoping)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md states 'Remediation tasks are proposed only for the 2.1.x stream. No remediation is needed for the 2.2.x stream.' Two tasks created: upstream backport (release/0.3.z) and downstream propagation (rhtpa-release.0.3.z), both targeting 2.1.x. No 2.2.x tasks exist.

assertion_results.text: The analysis treats the issue as unscoped (no stream suffix) and checks ALL versions across all streams (Step 1 Stream scope resolution, unscoped path)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md states 'Stream scope: Unscoped (no stream suffix) — analyze all streams' and lists both 2.1.x and 2.2.x streams. version-impact.md table includes all versions across both streams (2.1.0, 2.1.1, 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4).

assertion_results.text: Cross-stream impact notice is NOT generated because the issue is unscoped — it covers all streams by definition (Step 8 Case B applies only to scoped issues)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md explicitly states 'Since the issue is unscoped (covers all streams), no cross-stream impact notice is needed — the version impact analysis already covers all streams.' No cross-stream impact notice appears in any output file.
